questions.scenario: Standard Step has a 30 second recast.
questions.question: How should you use Standard Step?
questions.explanation: Standard Step should be used on cooldown. It grants a party damage buff - maximum uptime means maximum party DPS.

questions.scenario: Technical Step has a 120 second cooldown.
questions.question: What makes Technical Step special?
questions.explanation: Technical Step is your 2-minute party buff. Align it with other raid buffs for maximum burst damage across the party.

questions.scenario: You're executing a dance. Buttons are glowing in sequence.
questions.question: How do you complete the dance?
options: Press glowing buttons in order shown
questions.explanation: Press the glowing step buttons in the order they appear. Standard has 2 steps, Technical has 4 steps. Then press Finish.

questions.scenario: You started a dance but got distracted by mechanics.
questions.question: What happens if the dance timer expires?
options: You lose the step and must restart
questions.explanation: Dances have timers. If you don't complete the steps in time, you lose progress and must start over. Finish dances promptly.

questions.scenario: Standard Finish grants a party damage buff.
questions.question: What's the buff duration?
questions.explanation: Standard Finish's buff lasts 30 seconds - exactly matching its cooldown. Using it on cooldown maintains permanent uptime.

questions.scenario: You just used Cascade.
questions.question: What proc might you get?
questions.explanation: Cascade can proc Silken Symmetry, which enables Reverse Cascade. Consuming procs grants Feathers.

questions.scenario: You used Fountain and got Silken Flow.
questions.question: What ability does Silken Flow enable?
questions.explanation: Silken Flow enables Fountainfall. Like Silken Symmetry/Reverse Cascade, consuming this proc grants a Feather.

questions.scenario: Flourish just came off cooldown.
questions.question: What does Flourish do?
questions.explanation: Flourish grants all your procs at once - Silken Symmetry, Silken Flow, Threefold Fan, and Fourfold Fan. Use during burst windows.

questions.scenario: You have Threefold Fan Dance and Fourfold Fan Dance procs.
questions.question: What do these enable?
questions.explanation: Threefold and Fourfold procs enable Fan Dance III and IV respectively. These are high-potency attacks from Flourish.

questions.scenario: You have Silken Symmetry with 5 seconds remaining.
questions.question: What should you prioritize?
options: Use Reverse Cascade before it expires
questions.explanation: Never let procs fall off - they're your main damage. Use Reverse Cascade before the proc expires to get the damage and Feather.

questions.scenario: Your Esprit gauge is building during combat.
questions.question: What generates Esprit?
options: Your and your partner's weapon skills
questions.explanation: Esprit generates from both your weapon skills and your Dance Partner's. A high-CPM partner generates Esprit faster.

questions.scenario: Saber Dance costs 50 Esprit.
questions.question: Why is Saber Dance important?
questions.explanation: Saber Dance is your highest potency GCD. Spend Esprit on it regularly - don't let the gauge cap at 100.

questions.scenario: Your Esprit is at 85 and still building.
questions.question: What should you do?
options: Use Saber Dance to prevent capping
questions.explanation: Use Saber Dance at 80+ Esprit to prevent overcapping. Any Esprit generated at 100 is wasted.

questions.scenario: Burst window is active. You have 60 Esprit.
questions.question: Should you use Saber Dance now?
questions.explanation: During burst windows, use Saber Dance even at 50+ Esprit. Getting more high-potency hits under raid buffs is worth it.

questions.scenario: You're choosing a Dance Partner.
questions.question: What type of job generates Esprit fastest?
questions.explanation: Partner a high-CPM (casts per minute) job like Ninja or Monk. More weapon skills = more Esprit generation for both of you.

questions.scenario: You have 4 Feathers and Silken Symmetry proc.
questions.question: What should you do first?
options: Fan Dance I first, then Reverse Cascade
questions.explanation: At 4 Feathers, you're capped. Use Fan Dance I first to make room, then Reverse Cascade won't waste a Feather.

questions.scenario: Fan Dance I consumes 1 Feather.
questions.question: What might Fan Dance I proc?
questions.explanation: Fan Dance I has a chance to proc Fan Dance III. Weave Fan Dance III immediately when it procs.

questions.scenario: You have 3 Feathers. Burst window starts in 10 seconds.
questions.question: Is 3 Feathers a good amount to enter burst with?
questions.explanation: Hold 3 Feathers going into burst to dump during Devilment. This maximizes oGCD damage under your personal buff.

questions.scenario: Feathers are capped at 4.
questions.question: How do you get Feathers?
questions.explanation: Feathers come from using Reverse Cascade and Fountainfall (consuming Silken Symmetry and Silken Flow procs).

questions.scenario: Flourish grants Fourfold Fan Dance.
questions.question: What is Fan Dance IV?
questions.explanation: Fan Dance IV from Flourish is high potency and should be used during burst. It's a priority oGCD during your burst window.

questions.scenario: Technical Finish is your 2-minute party buff.
questions.question: What's the correct burst opener sequence?
questions.explanation: Technical Finish first (party buff), then Devilment (personal buff), then Flourish (procs). This stacks buffs for maximum damage.

questions.scenario: Devilment grants 15% crit/DH.
questions.question: Who benefits from Devilment?
questions.explanation: Devilment's crit/direct hit buff applies to both you AND your Dance Partner. Partner wisely for double value.

questions.scenario: Other DPS are announcing their 2-minute buffs.
questions.question: How should you handle Technical Step timing?
questions.explanation: Technical Finish is a party buff. Coordinate with other 2-minute raid buffs (Battle Litany, Chain Stratagem, etc.) for stacked value.

questions.scenario: Flourish has a 60 second cooldown.
questions.question: When should you use Flourish?
questions.explanation: Flourish every burst window (60s aligns with every other minute). It grants all your procs for maximum burst damage.

questions.scenario: Entering burst window.
questions.question: What resources should you have ready?
questions.explanation: Enter burst with 3 Feathers and 50+ Esprit. This lets you dump Saber Dance and Fan Dances under Technical/Devilment.

questions.scenario: Devilment grants Starfall Dance proc.
questions.question: What's the priority of Starfall Dance?
questions.explanation: Starfall Dance is 600 potency and your highest priority proc during Devilment. Use it before the buff expires.

questions.scenario: Standard Finish grants Finishing Move.
questions.question: How much potency is Finishing Move?
questions.explanation: Finishing Move is 850 potency - extremely powerful. Use it during your burst window for maximum value under buffs.

questions.scenario: You used Finishing Move.
questions.question: What chains from Finishing Move?
questions.explanation: Last Dance follows Finishing Move as a 520 potency instant. It's a chain combo - don't miss the follow-up.

questions.scenario: Technical Finish was just used.
questions.question: What proc does Technical Finish grant?
questions.explanation: Technical Finish grants Tillana, a 600 potency instant. Use it during your burst window - it's unique to Technical.

questions.scenario: Devilment is ending in 3 seconds. Starfall Dance unused.
questions.question: What should you prioritize?
questions.explanation: Starfall Dance is only available during Devilment. If it's about to end, use Starfall immediately or lose it entirely.

questions.scenario: You're selecting a Dance Partner.
questions.question: Who makes the best partner?
questions.explanation: Partner the highest DPS player - typically SAM, NIN, or MNK. They benefit most from Devilment and generate Esprit fastest.

questions.scenario: Your Dance Partner died.
questions.question: What should you do?
questions.explanation: Switch your partner to another DPS immediately. An alive partner generating Esprit and receiving Devilment > waiting for a raise.

questions.scenario: A raidwide is coming in 5 seconds.
questions.question: What should you use?
questions.explanation: Shield Samba gives 15% party damage reduction for 15 seconds. Use it before raidwides to reduce incoming damage.

questions.scenario: Improvisation is available.
questions.question: What does Improvisation provide?
questions.explanation: Improvisation provides party healing over time and builds a barrier. Use it during downtime or before heavy damage.

questions.scenario: Another DNC in the party also has Shield Samba.
questions.question: How should you coordinate?
options: Only one DNC needs to use it
questions.explanation: Coordinate Shield Samba via IPC or communication. Alternate usage to cover more raidwides rather than stacking on one.
